matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a COMT catecholamine-metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal signaling, pain/stress traits, methyl-donor use, or pharmacogenetic research. It should not be treated as a deterministic personality or psychiatric call.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs4680 (COMT Val158Met) dosage suggests this sample carries the COMT prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a COMT catecholamine-metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal signaling, pain/stress traits, methyl-donor use, or pharmacogenetic research. It should not be treated as a deterministic personality or psychiatric call. This specific marker is curated as COMT catecholamine-metabolism activity marker. rs4680 (COMT Val158Met) is curated as a COMT marker for Catecholamine metabolism and prefrontal dopamine tone, Pain, stress, and cognition association research. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the catecholamine methylation, dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal neurotransmitter tone, and methyl-donor use pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs4680 (COMT Val158Met) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the COMT prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag catecholamine methylation, dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal neurotransmitter tone, and methyl-donor use.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a COMT catecholamine-metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal signaling, pain/stress traits, methyl-donor use, or pharmacogenetic research. It should not be treated as a deterministic personality or psychiatric call. An observed COMT variant places this sample in the curated COMT research context. The local COMT bundle is neurotransmitter-metabolism oriented. Val158Met is a common functional marker but remains a probabilistic research modifier, not a deterministic behavior or psychiatric predictor. Val158Met alters COMT enzyme thermostability/activity and is widely studied in dopamine, pain, stress, cognition, and medication-response contexts.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Catecholamine metabolism and prefrontal dopamine tone
Pain, stress, and cognition association research
Pharmacogenetic and neuropsychiatric context

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a COMT variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a COMT catecholamine-metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal signaling, pain/stress traits, methyl-donor use, or pharmacogenetic research. It should not be treated as a deterministic personality or psychiatric call. COMT methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a catecholamine methylation, dopamine/norepinephrine turnover, prefrontal neurotransmitter tone, and methyl-donor use gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local COMT bundle is neurotransmitter-metabolism oriented. Val158Met is a common functional marker but remains a probabilistic research modifier, not a deterministic behavior or psychiatric predictor. Val158Met alters COMT enzyme thermostability/activity and is widely studied in dopamine, pain, stress, cognition, and medication-response contexts.
